title: State Machine

statement.markdown:
## Finite State Machine
A finite state machine (FSM) is a mathematical model that can be in one of a finite number of states. It can change state, according to some set of rules, as it reacts to some inputs. Implement a finite state machine in TypeScript with the following requirements:

### Requirements
1. The FSM should have a state machine with the following states:
   - idle: the default state
   - playing: the state when the music is being played
   - paused: the state when the music is paused
2. The FSM should support transitions between states:
   - From idle to playing: play music
   - From playing to paused: pause music
   - From paused to playing: resume music from where it was paused
   - From idle to paused: pause music from the start
3. The FSM should have a method to report the current state of the machine

constraints: Do not use any external libraries
Do not implement any UI components or user interaction
Keep the implementation simple, readable and well-documented
